Then it mentions that most lightning flashes take place inside a cloud, and only a few can be seen jumping between two clouds or between earth and a cloud. Then there's a sentence about 2,000 thunderstorms every minute producing about 100 lightning strikes on earth every second. The passage says there are about 100 lightning strikes on earth every second. So if A says only a small number occur on earth, that's not true because 100 per second is a lot. So A is probably incorrect. B) Lightning flashes usually jump from one cloud to another. The passage states that most lightning flashes take place inside a cloud, and only a few between clouds or earth and cloud. So "usually" would mean most of the time. If most are inside clouds, then B is not correct because "jumping from one cloud to another" would be the between-cloud ones, which are a few. So B is false. C) Lightning travels 5 times faster than thunder. The passage mentions that light from lightning reaches the eyes almost instantly, while sound takes about 5 seconds per mile.
